title: How I'd Succeed as a Marathon Sales Exec
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: A 14-slide sales strategy presentation delivered by Aberdeen Group Chief Research Officer Peter S. Kastner to Marathon Technologies in April 1998. The deck argues that the NT high-availability market is ripe for Marathon's Endurance product, that named competitors (Stratus, Tandem/Compaq, Microsoft Cluster Server) are weak or misaligned, and that the real obstacle is customer inertia. It prescribes a go-to-market plan based on FUD around downtime costs, alliance-building with Microsoft field sales and Big Six systems integrators, and vertical market focus across retail, healthcare, public safety, utilities, financial services, and ISPs.
importance_rationale: Captures a practitioner-analyst's candid, first-person sales strategy advice during the late-1990s Windows NT high-availability market formation period; rare primary source of analyst-as-consultant voice.
relevance_rationale: Directly documents competitive positioning, channel strategy, and vertical market priorities for fault-tolerant/high-availability computing on NT circa 1998 — a pivotal transitional moment before Windows 2000 and commodity clustering matured.
prescience_rationale: Correctly identified Microsoft NT ecosystem partners (BackOffice VARs, Big Six SIs) as the right channel; correctly anticipated ISP and e-commerce workloads as growth drivers; accurately flagged customer inertia (not named competitors) as the primary barrier to adoption.
